questions.question: What is statistics?
questions.correct_answer: A branch of mathematics dealing with collection, organization, analysis, and interpretation of data
questions.explanation: It helps in making sense of numerical information in real-life situations.

questions.question: Define raw data.
questions.correct_answer: Unprocessed data collected directly from observation or survey
questions.explanation: Raw data is in its original form and needs to be organized for analysis.

questions.question: What is meant by grouped data?
questions.correct_answer: Data that is arranged in classes or intervals
questions.explanation: Grouping makes it easier to summarize and interpret large data sets.

questions.question: What is a frequency distribution table?
questions.correct_answer: A table showing classes/values and the number of times each occurs
questions.explanation: It organizes data to show how frequently each value appears.

questions.question: Define class interval.
questions.correct_answer: The difference between upper and lower boundaries of a class
questions.explanation: Represents the range of values grouped together in a class.

questions.question: Define class mark.
questions.correct_answer: Mid-point of a class interval = (Lower limit + Upper limit)/2
questions.explanation: Used to represent all data values in that class.

questions.question: What is the mean of ungrouped data?
questions.correct_answer: Sum of all observations divided by number of observations
questions.explanation: It gives the average value of the data set.

questions.question: How is mean calculated for grouped data?
questions.correct_answer: Mean = Σ(f × x)/Σf, where f = frequency, x = class mark
questions.explanation: Weighted average using class marks as representatives of each class.

questions.question: Define median.
questions.correct_answer: The middle value of an ordered data set
questions.explanation: It divides the data into two equal halves.

questions.question: Define mode.
questions.correct_answer: The value that occurs most frequently in the data set
questions.explanation: Represents the value with highest occurrence.

notes.title: Statistics Basics
notes.content: Statistics involves collection, organization, representation, and interpretation of data.

notes.title: Grouped vs Ungrouped Data
notes.content: Grouped data is arranged in classes; ungrouped data is raw and unorganized.

notes.title: Frequency Table
notes.content: Organizes data into classes and their frequencies for easy analysis.

notes.title: Measures of Central Tendency
notes.content: Mean, median, and mode describe the central point of data.

facts.fact: Sum of frequencies = total number of observations.
Class mark represents all values in a class for calculation purposes.
Mean gives the average; median divides data into halves; mode shows most frequent value.
Grouped data is used when there are too many observations to list individually.
Median and mode are less affected by extreme values compared to mean.
